perturbation.original_text: Mentor shall notify Organiser immediately upon discovery of any unauthorised use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Mentor, and will cooperate with 7 Organiser in every reasonable way to help Organiser regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorised use or publication.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor shall cooperate with 7 Organiser in every reasonable way to help Organiser regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorised use or publication. Mentor shall notify Organiser immediately upon discovery of any unauthorised use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Mentor.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the notification requirement to the end diminishes the urgency of reporting breaches, potentially violating data breach notification laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 33 of the GDPR requires data controllers to notify the relevant supervisory authority of a personal data breach without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it. Delaying the notification obligation, as done in the modified text, directly contradicts the GDPR's requirement for prompt notification.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Art. 33 GDPR – Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority - General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Search for: Skip to content Search for: Imprint | Privacy Policy | Liability 1 In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority competent in accordance with Article 55 , unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons. 2 Where the notification to the supervisory authority is not made within 72 hours, it shall be accompanied by reasons for the delay. The processor shall notify the controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a personal data breach. The notification referred to in paragraph 1 shall at least: describe the nature of the personal data breach including where possible, the categories and approximate number of data subjects concerned and the categories and approximate number of personal data records concerned; communicate the name and contact details of the data protection officer or other contact point where more information can be obtained; describe the likely consequences of the personal data breach; describe the measures taken or proposed to be taken by the controller to address the personal data breach, including, where appropriate, measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects. Where, and in so far as, it is not possible to provide the information at the same time, the information may be provided in phases without undue further delay. 1 The controller shall document any personal data breaches, comprising the facts relating to the personal data breach, its effects and the remedial action taken. 2 That documentation shall enable the supervisory authority to verify compliance with this Article. Suitable Recitals ( 85 ) Notification Obligation of Breaches to the Supervisory Authority ( 87 ) Promp
perturbation.justification: The changed text reorders the sentence about reporting breaches, delaying the notification obligation. This creates a legal contradiction. According to Art. 33 GDPR, 'In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority'. By placing the notification requirement after the cooperation clause, the changed text weakens the 'without undue delay' requirement, therefore contradicting the GDPR's mandate for prompt notification. The original text places more emphasis on the immediate notification, while the changed text diminishes it.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the material laws of the Federal Republic of Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the material laws of the Federal Republic of Germany. The parties agree that any disputes will be subject to mediation in Antarctica.
perturbation.explanation: Adding an irrelevant and impractical clause about mediation in Antarctica creates a contradiction about the governing jurisdiction and enforcement mechanisms.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 134 of the BGB states a juridical act which violates a statutory prohibition is void unless the statute provides otherwise. By stating the governing law is Germany, the contract implies German civil code applies. Requiring mediation in Antarctica introduces an element that is legally and practically unenforceable under German law, contradicting the intent of choosing German law as governing.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction by specifying mediation in Antarctica while also stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany. According to the Law Explanation, this creates a conflict as the practical enforceability of mediation in Antarctica under German law is questionable. The BGB § 134 states that a juridical act violating a statutory prohibition is void, unless the statute provides otherwise. The introduction of mediation in Antarctica, practically unenforceable under German law, undermines the initial selection of German law as the governing law, creating a structural flaw rendering the contract potentially void under German law.

perturbation.original_text: The place of juri sdiction for any and all legal disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement is Munich (Landgericht München I). Imperative places of jurisdiction under German Law shall remain unaffected by the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: The place of juri sdiction for any and all legal disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement is Munich (Landgericht München I). Imperative places of jurisdiction under German Law shall remain unaffected by the foregoing. The parties agree to resolve the disputes by coin flip.
perturbation.explanation: Adding that disputes should be solved by coin flip introduces an unenforcable method of dispute resolution, that is not based on the governing law stated, which is German law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 2 of the ZPO states that courts have jurisdiction only if provided by law. Coin flip is not a legal method of dispute resolution, thus it contradicts the section on jurisdiction within the ZPO, and is thus unenforceable.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a method of dispute resolution (coin flip) that directly contradicts the standard legal processes implied within German Law, as the original text states it should follow. The LLM explanation points to the ZPO, however the snippets provided discuss the 'value' of the dispute, which does not add to or detract from the analysis that the addition of a coin flip introduces a non-legal, and thus unenforceable, resolution to disputes, creating a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction. The snippets do not help in this analysis.

perturbation.original_text: Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. The Organiser will deduct 50% of the fees paid to the mentor and donate that to charity. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction about payment terms. It first says that no deductions will be made, but then says that 50% will be deducted for charity.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 311 BGB states that a legal obligation is created by a contract. In this case, the contract outlines the payment terms. By introducing a contradiction stating that deductions will and will not be made, the original terms of the contract become void, because it is unclear.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 311 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 311 Rechtsgeschäftliche und rechtsgeschäftsähnliche Schuldverhältnisse (1) Zur Begründung eines Schuldverhältnisses durch Rechtsgeschäft sowie zur Änderung des Inhalts eines Schuldverhältnisses ist ein Vertrag zwischen den Beteiligten erforderlich, soweit nicht das Gesetz ein anderes vorschreibt. (2) Ein Schuldverhältnis mit Pflichten nach § 241 Abs. 2 entsteht auch durch 1. die Aufnahme von Vertragsverhandlungen, 2. die Anbahnung eines Vertrags, bei welcher der eine Teil im Hinblick auf eine etwaige rechtsgeschäftliche Beziehung dem anderen Teil die Möglichkeit zur Einwirkung auf seine Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen gewährt oder ihm diese anvertraut, oder 3. ähnliche geschäftliche Kontakte. (3) Ein Schuldverhältnis mit Pflichten nach § 241 Abs. 2 kann auch zu Personen entstehen, die nicht selbst Vertragspartei werden sollen. Ein solches Schuldverhältnis entsteht insbesondere, wenn der Dritte in besonderem Maße Vertrauen für sich in Anspruch nimmt und dadurch die Vertragsverhandlungen oder den Vertragsschluss erheblich beeinflusst. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction regarding deductions from the mentor's fees. The original text states "No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor", while the changed text specifies that "The Organiser will deduct 50% of the fees paid to the mentor and donate that to charity." This is a structural flaw creating a legal contradiction as it makes the payment terms unclear and unenforceable, violating the principle that contracts should clearly define the obligations of each party.
